feat(secrets): Implement secrets & credential loading module (bd-1jt.7.4)

- Add secrets.sh with token resolution (DSR_GH_TOKEN > GITHUB_TOKEN > GH_TOKEN > gh auth)
- Implement secrets_redact() for masking tokens in strings
- Implement secrets_redact_json() for JSON output redaction
- Add GitHub scope validation (contents:write, workflow)
- Add Slack/Discord webhook validation
- Add secrets_doctor_summary() for doctor integration
- Add comprehensive unit tests (19 passing)

Security: All token values are masked before logging.
Redaction patterns cover GitHub tokens, Slack/Discord webhooks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.3","title":"Implement dsr repos command - manage tool registry","description":"# Implement dsr repos command — manage tool registry

## Purpose
Manage the repo registry used by build/release/install. This is the authoritative source for targets, commands, and workflow mappings.

## Registry Layout (XDG)
`~/.config/dsr/repos.d/<tool>.yaml` (one file per tool)
- `schema_version: "1.0.0"`
- `repo`, `local_path`, `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets`, `workflow`, `act_job_map`, `checks`
- `artifact_naming`, `archive_format`, `enabled`

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr repos list
 dsr repos add <tool>
 dsr repos remove <tool>
 dsr repos info <tool> [--json]
 dsr repos validate
 dsr repos discover [--apply]
```

## Behavior
- Reads/writes `repos.d/` (merge all tool files deterministically)
- Validates required fields (repo, build_cmd, targets, workflow jobs)
- `discover` scans `/data/projects` for Cargo.toml/go.mod/package.json
- Discovery is **read-only** unless `--apply`
- Never creates new directories; discovery is **read-only** unless `--apply`
- Never uses global `cd` for git ops; use `git -C <path>`
- Uses `yq` when available for safe edits; otherwise read-only fallback

## Best-Practice Notes
- Normalize paths (absolute or `~`), reject relative
- Use guardrails for any file deletion (`repos remove`)
- stdout for JSON/paths, stderr for human output only

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List shows all tools with language + targets
- [ ] Add/remove works and persists per-tool files
- [ ] Info shows complete build config
- [ ] Validation catches missing fields
- [ ] Discover finds repos without mutating config unless confirmed
- [ ] Registry uses `repos.d` (no monolithic `repos.yaml`)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:02.877692893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:07:51.168912408Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:06:16.702769732Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr repos command with list, add, remove, info, validate, discover, and sync subcommands.  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.4","title":"Implement dsr fallback command - trigger local build pipeline","description":"# Implement `dsr fallback` — Orchestrated Build + Release

## Purpose
End-to-end fallback pipeline when GH Actions is throttled: build, verify, release, and report.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr fallback <tool>
 dsr fallback <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr fallback <tool> --build-only
 dsr fallback <tool> --skip-checks
 dsr fallback <tool> --dry-run
 dsr fallback <tool> --resume
```

## Flow (must be deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (build from tag)
2. Run pre-release checks (unless --skip-checks)
3. Acquire lock + create run_id
4. Build all targets (act + native hosts)
5. Generate manifest + checksums + signatures
6. Verify artifacts
7. Create release and upload assets
8. Emit JSON summary + status report

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works end-to-end without manual intervention
- [ ] Uses manifest for release validation
- [ ] Supports resume after partial failure
- [ ] Clear logs + JSON output (schema-compliant)
- [ ] --dry-run outputs a deterministic plan without side  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.5","title":"Create default repos.d registry with all 16 tools configured","description":"# Create Default repos.d Registry with All 16 Tools Configured

## Purpose
Ship a complete, ready-to-use repository registry so `dsr` can build/release/install the full Dicklesworthstone toolchain without manual setup.

## Why this matters
Most commands (`check`, `build`, `release`, installers) rely on accurate repo metadata. Missing or inconsistent entries cause brittle behavior and user confusion.

## Location
`~/.config/dsr/repos.d/` (created by `dsr config init`)
- One file per tool: `repos.d/<tool>.yaml`
- Each file contains `schema_version` + tool fields

## Required Fields (per tool)
- `repo` (owner/name)
- `local_path` (absolute path, usually under `/data/projects`, but allow overrides)
- `language` (rust|go|bun|node)
- `binary_name`
- `build_cmd` (reproducible, non-interactive)
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (lint/test/typecheck commands)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`
- `enabled` (bool) to allow opt-out

## Guardrails
- Never create new directories under `/data/projects`
- Missing `local_path` → warn, do not mutate
- Use `yq` for safe writes when available; read-only fallback otherwise

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 16 tools configured with full metadata
- [ ] Paths validated (existence check + absolute)
- [ ] Workflows + job mappings verified
- [ ] Artifact naming matches installer expectations
- [ ] Registry lives in `repos.d/` (no monolithic `repos.yaml`)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:14:13.167130536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:03.116054165Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:33.023057790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.7","title":"Implement auto-tag from version files","description":"# Auto-Tag from Version Files

## Purpose
Automatically create git tags when version files (Cargo.toml, package.json, VERSION, etc.) are updated. This keeps releases consistent across tools and avoids manual tagging errors.

## Version Sources (configurable)
| Language | File | Pattern |
|----------|------|---------|
| Rust | Cargo.toml | version = "X.Y.Z" |
| Go | VERSION or version.go | Version = "X.Y.Z" |
| Bun/Node | package.json | "version": "X.Y.Z" |
| Python | pyproject.toml | version = "X.Y.Z" |

## Implementation Notes
- Use repo registry (`repos.d/<tool>.yaml`) to resolve `local_path`
- **No global cd** — use `git -C <repo>`
- Dirty tree detection via exit codes (`git diff-index --quiet HEAD --`)
- Tag existence via `git show-ref --tags -d "refs/tags/vX.Y.Z"`
- Support `--dry-run` and `--non-interactive`

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr version detect <tool>   # Show current version
dsr version tag <tool>      # Create tag if needed
dsr version tag --all       # Tag all repos with new versions
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect version from all supported language files
- [ ] Skip if tag already exists
- [ ] Create annotated tags
- [ ] Push tags to remote
- [ ] All git operations use `git -C` (no global cd)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:57.429934130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:12.508139810Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.309988979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:54.300293741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.8","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management (XDG + Schema Validation)

## Purpose
Centralize configuration so every command uses the same sources of truth for repos, hosts, secrets, and defaults. Consistent config prevents drift and makes automation reproducible.

## XDG Layout
- `~/.config/dsr/`
  - `config.yaml` (global defaults + schema_version)
  - `repos.yaml` (tool registry + build matrix)
  - `hosts.yaml` (build hosts + capabilities)
  - `notifications.yaml`
- `~/.cache/dsr/` (API cache, downloads)
- `~/.local/state/dsr/` (logs, state, run history)

## Precedence Rules
1. CLI flags
2. Environment variables (`DSR_*`)
3. Config files
4. Built-in defaults

## repos.yaml Schema (minimum fields)
- `tool`, `repo`, `local_path`
- `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (pre-release gates)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`

## hosts.yaml Schema
- host aliases, platform, capabilities, concurrency limits
- ssh options (connect timeout, identity)

## Migration & Versioning
- `schema_version` required
- `dsr config migrate` upgrades older schemas
- Unknown keys rejected unless `--allow-unknown`

## Commands
```bash
dsr config init
 dsr config show [key]
 dsr config validate
 dsr config set KEY=VALUE
 dsr config edit
 dsr config migrate
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config loads at startup with deterministic precedence
- [ ] Validation catches missing/invalid keys
- [ ] Defaults are sane and documented
- [ ] `--json` outputs resolved config for automation
- [ ] yq-based edits are safe; fallback mode is read-only","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:27.935807062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:12.840449840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:47:07.588658283Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr config command with init/show/get/set/validate/edit subcommands.  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.9","title":"Implement GitHub API adapter with caching + rate-limit handling","description":"# Implement GitHub API Adapter with Caching + Rate-Limit Handling

## Purpose
Centralize all GitHub API access so commands are consistent, cached, and resilient to rate limits.

## Responsibilities
- Prefer gh api when available (auth + pagination)
- Fallback to curl + GITHUB_TOKEN
- Support ETag / If-None-Match caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/
- Normalize errors into a common JSON shape
- Expose helpers for workflow runs, releases, tags, and compare endpoints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unified helper used by dsr check/watch/release
- [ ] API responses cached with TTL + ETag
- [ ] Rate-limit errors trigger backoff + clear messages
- [ ] All errors map to global exit codes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413349290Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413268548Z","close_reason":"Implemented GitHub API adapter in src/github.sh with: gh_api() for unified API access (gh CLI or curl fallback), ETag caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/, rate-limit handling with backoff, and helpers for workflow_runs, releases, tags, compare, and asset upload.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.3","title":"Setup macOS build environment on Mac mini (mmini)","description":"# Setup macOS Build Environment on Mac mini

## Purpose
Ensure the Mac mini (mmini) has all toolchains needed to build macOS binaries.

## Host Details
- Alias: mmini
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS (Apple Silicon ARM64)
- User: jemanuel
- Sudo: Passwordless via /etc/sudoers.d/jemanuel

## Required Toolchains

### 1. Homebrew (Package Manager)
\`\`\`bash
# Should already be installed, verify:
brew --version

# If missing:
/bin/bash -c "\$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
\`\`\`

### 2. Rust Nightly
\`\`\`bash
# Install rustup
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y

# Install nightly
rustup install nightly
rustup default nightly

# Add x86_64 target for universal builds
rustup target add x86_64-apple-darwin
\`\`\`

### 3. Go (Latest)
\`\`\`bash
brew install go
# or download from go.dev for specific version
\`\`\`

### 4. Bun (Latest)
\`\`\`bash
brew install oven-sh/bun/bun
# or
curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
\`\`\`

### 5. Xcode Command Line Tools
\`\`\`bash
xcode-select --install
# Needed for: clang, ar, ranlib, etc.
\`\`\`

## Universal Binary Support
macOS can build both ARM64 and x86_64:

### Rust Universal Binary
\`\`\`bash
# Build for both architectures
cargo build --release --target aarch64-apple-darwin
cargo build --release --target x86_64-apple-darwin

# Create universal binary with lipo
lipo -create \\
  target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/mytool \\
  target/x86_64-apple-darwin/release/mytool \\
  -output mytool-universal
\`\`\`

### Go Universal Binary
\`\`\`bash
# Build ARM64
GOARCH=arm64 go build -o mytool-arm64 ./cmd/mytool

# Build x86_64
GOARCH=amd64 go build -o mytool-amd64 ./cmd/mytool

# Create universal
lipo -create mytool-arm64 mytool-amd64 -output mytool-universal
\`\`\`

## Verification Script
Create ~/bin/dsr-verify-toolchains.sh:
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== DSR Toolchain Verification (macOS) ==="
echo -n "Homebrew: "; brew --version | head -1 || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Rust: "; rustc --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Cargo: "; cargo --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Go: "; go version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Bun: "; bun --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Xcode CLT: "; xcode-select -p || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "lipo: "; which lipo || echo "MISSING"
echo "=== Rust Targets ==="
rustup target list --installed
echo "=== Done ==="
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All toolchains installed and in PATH
- [ ] Rust can build for both ARM64 and x86_64
- [ ] Go can cross-compile for both architectures
- [ ] lipo available for creating universal binaries
- [ ] Verification script exits 0

## Notes
- Mac mini is accessed via Tailscale, ensure daemon is running
- Apple Silicon can run x86_64 binaries via Rosetta 2
- Consider code signing for distribution (future enhancement)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:08.613890651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:29.631435925Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:08.613890651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.4","title":"CRITICAL: Use nektos/act to run GH Actions YAML locally","description":"# Use nektos/act to Run GH Actions Workflows Locally

## Purpose
Reuse existing `.github/workflows/*.yml` to build releases locally when GH Actions is throttled.

## Key `act` Capabilities (from official docs)
- Run a specific workflow: `act -W .github/workflows/release.yml`
- Run a specific job: `act -j build-linux`
- List jobs: `act -l`
- Override runner images: `-P ubuntu-latest=...`
- Set container architecture: `--container-architecture linux/arm64`
- Control pulls / offline mode: `--pull=false` / `--action-offline-mode`
- Route artifacts: `--artifact-server-path /tmp/act-artifacts`

## Critical Limitations
- `act` runs workflows in **Docker** (Linux). It **cannot** execute macOS/Windows runner jobs.
- macOS/Windows builds must be native (mmini, wlap) or cross-compiled.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `act` runs `release.yml` for Linux targets
- [ ] Runner images pinned via `~/.actrc`
- [ ] Artifact collection path standardized
- [ ] Offline mode works once images are cached

## References

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.5","title":"Install and configure act on Linux host (trj)","description":"# Install and Configure act on Linux Host (trj)

## Purpose
Install `act` where Linux workflows will run locally. `act` runs Linux containers only and is required on the Linux build host.

## Linux Host (trj)
- Install Docker + act
- Configure `~/.actrc` (runner image, artifact path, container arch)
- Pre-pull runner images to avoid CI delays

## Optional: macOS Host (mmini)
- Install act via Homebrew if you want local workflow debugging
- Use Colima or Docker Desktop for Linux containers
- Set `--container-architecture linux/amd64` when running x86 workflows

## Important Limitation
`act` cannot execute macOS/Windows runner jobs; those builds remain native on mmini/wlap.

- [ ] Docker daemon accessible for act
- [ ] Runner images pinned and cached

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.7","title":"Setup Windows build environment on Windows laptop (wlap)","description":"# Setup Windows Build Environment on Windows Laptop

## Purpose
Enable native Windows builds via SSH to a Windows laptop, avoiding cross-compilation limitations and ensuring native binary quality.

## Why Native Windows Builds?

Cross-compilation from Linux has limitations:
- Rust: cargo-zigbuild works but can miss Windows-specific optimizations
- Go: Cross-compile works well but some CGO dependencies fail
- Bun: Limited cross-compile support

Native Windows builds provide:
- Proper Windows SDK integration
- Native MSVC optimizations
- Accurate testing environment
- Full Windows-specific feature support

## Prerequisites on Windows Laptop

### 1. Enable SSH Server
\`\`\`powershell
# Run as Administrator
Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0
Start-Service sshd
Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType 'Automatic'

# Allow through firewall
New-NetFirewallRule -Name 'SSH' -DisplayName 'OpenSSH Server' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort 22
\`\`\`

### 2. Configure SSH Key Auth
\`\`\`powershell
# Create .ssh directory
mkdir ~\.ssh

# Add authorized keys (copy from trj)
notepad ~\.ssh\authorized_keys
# Paste the public key from trj
\`\`\`

### 3. Install Build Toolchains

#### Rust
\`\`\`powershell
# Download and run rustup-init.exe
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://win.rustup.rs -OutFile rustup-init.exe
.\rustup-init.exe -y --default-toolchain nightly
# Restart shell
\`\`\`

#### Go
\`\`\`powershell
# Download from go.dev or use winget
winget install GoLang.Go
# Or chocolatey
choco install golang
\`\`\`

#### Bun
\`\`\`powershell
powershell -c "irm bun.sh/install.ps1 | iex"
\`\`\`

#### Visual Studio Build Tools (for Rust MSVC)
\`\`\`powershell
# Download Visual Studio Build Tools
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
# Select "Desktop development with C++" workload
\`\`\`

## SSH Configuration on trj/mmini

Add to ~/.ssh/config:
\`\`\`
Host wlap
    HostName <windows-laptop-ip>
    User <username>
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/wlap_ed25519
    # May need PowerShell as default shell
    # RemoteCommand powershell.exe
\`\`\`

Generate key:
\`\`\`bash
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/wlap_ed25519 -N ""
ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/wlap_ed25519 wlap
\`\`\`

## Build Commands via SSH

### Rust
\`\`\`bash
ssh wlap "cd C:\projects\cass && cargo build --release"
scp wlap:C:\projects\cass\target\release\cass.exe ./builds/
\`\`\`

### Go
\`\`\`bash
ssh wlap "cd C:\projects\ntm && go build -o ntm.exe ./cmd/ntm"
scp wlap:C:\projects\ntm\ntm.exe ./builds/
\`\`\`

## Updated Build Matrix

| Target | Host | Method |
|--------|------|--------|
| linux-amd64 | trj | native |
| linux-arm64 | trj | cross-compile |
| darwin-arm64 | mmini | native |
| darwin-amd64 | mmini | native |
| windows-amd64 | wlap | native |

## Verification Script
\`\`\`powershell
# C:\bin\dsr-verify-toolchains.ps1
Write-Host "=== DSR Toolchain Verification (Windows) ==="
Write-Host "Rust: " -NoNewline; rustc --version
Write-Host "Cargo: " -NoNewline; cargo --version
Write-Host "Go: " -NoNewline; go version
Write-Host "Bun: " -NoNewline; bun --version
Write-Host "MSVC: " -NoNewline; cl 2>&1 | Select-String "Version"
Write-Host "=== Done ==="
\`\`\`

## Networking Considerations
- If laptop is on different network, may need VPN or Tailscale
- Consider Tailscale for consistent IP across networks
- Test SSH from trj before relying on it

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH from trj to wlap works
- [ ] Rust builds produce valid .exe
- [ ] Go builds produce valid .exe
- [ ] File transfer (scp) works
- [ ] Verification script passes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:16:39.813118424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:59:11.231567149Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:16:39.813118424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.8","title":"Implement host health checking before builds","description":"# Host Health Checking Before Builds

## Purpose
Fail fast if a host is offline or missing required capabilities. Avoid long SSH hangs and late build failures.

## Checks
- SSH connectivity (short timeout + BatchMode)
- Disk space threshold
- Toolchains (rust/go/bun)
- Docker/Colima for act (if Linux builds run on host)
- Host clock drift (optional, warn only)

## Output
- JSON summary for automation
- Cache results for short TTL (5m)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH check completes quickly
- [ ] Toolchains verified per host capabilities
- [ ] Clear warnings for low disk or missing deps
- [ ] Build uses only healthy hosts unless overridden","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:11.872176622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:55.107460331Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:55.107442968Z","close_reason":"Integrated host_health.sh module into dsr CLI.  {"id":"bd-1jt.2.9","title":"Implement retry and recovery logic for failed builds","description":"# Retry and Recovery Logic

## Purpose
Handle transient failures gracefully without losing state or corrupting artifacts.

## Strategy
- Exponential backoff + jitter (avoid thundering herd)
- Only retry idempotent steps (ssh, download, upload)
- Persist state to resume after process crash

## Integration
- Uses build lock/state machine (bd-1jt.2.11)
- Skips completed hosts on resume
- Partial failures do not block successful hosts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Transient failures auto-recover
- [ ] State tracked persistently
- [ ] --resume works correctly
- [ ] Clear error reporting with logs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:53.592266937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:38.910863048Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:53.592266937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.971484244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:16.822551477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3","title":"Theme 3: Release Upload Automation","description":"# Release Upload Automation

## Purpose
After local builds complete, create GitHub releases and upload binaries with proper checksums and signatures.

## The dsr release Command
```bash
dsr release <tool> <version>           # Create release, upload all builds
dsr release <tool> <version> --draft   # Create as draft first
dsr release <tool> <version> --notes "..." # Custom release notes
```

## Release Workflow

### 1. Verify Builds Exist
```bash
# Check all expected artifacts exist
build_dir="$DSR_STATE_DIR/builds/$tool/$version"
for target in linux-amd64 linux-arm64 darwin-arm64 darwin-amd64 windows-amd64; do
  artifact="$build_dir/${tool}-${version}-${target}"
  [ -f "$artifact" ] || die "Missing: $artifact"
done
```

### 2. Generate Checksums (No global cd)
```bash
# Generate checksums in place
sha256sum "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-* > "$build_dir/${tool}-${version}-checksums.sha256"
```

### 3. Sign Artifacts (Optional but Recommended)
```bash
# Using minisign
for f in "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-*; do
  minisign -Sm "$f" -s ~/.minisign/key.sec
done
```

### 4. Create GitHub Release
```bash
gh release create "$version" \
  --repo "Dicklesworthstone/$tool" \
  --title "$tool $version" \
  --notes "$release_notes" \
  "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-*
```

### 5. Verify Upload
```bash
gh release view "$version" --repo "Dicklesworthstone/$tool" --json assets
```

## Release Notes Generation (Git Plumbing)
```bash
repo_dir="/data/projects/$tool"
last_tag=$(git -C "$repo_dir" describe --tags --abbrev=0 HEAD^)
git -C "$repo_dir" log --oneline ${last_tag}..HEAD > "$build_dir/release_notes.txt"
```

## Asset Naming Convention
```
{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}[.exe]
{tool}-{version}-checksums.sha256
{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}.minisig
```

Examples:
```
ntm-v1.5.1-linux-amd64
ntm-v1.5.1-darwin-arm64
ntm-v1.5.1-windows-amd64.exe
ntm-v1.5.1-checksums.sha256
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates release with correct tag
- [ ] All binaries uploaded and downloadable
- [ ] Checksums file included
- [ ] Release notes auto-generated if not provided
- [ ] Works for all 16 tools

## Error Handling
- Release already exists → Prompt to update or abort
- Upload fails → Retry, then report which assets failed
- Tag doesn't exist → Create tag or error","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:26.299668699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:49:45.249464757Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:26.299668699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.1","title":"Implement dsr release command - upload artifacts to GitHub","description":"# Implement `dsr release` — Upload Artifacts to GitHub

## Purpose
Create a GitHub Release from locally built artifacts, with retries, verification, and consistent metadata.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr release <tool> <version>
 dsr release <tool> <version> --artifacts <dir>
 dsr release <tool> <version> --draft
 dsr release <tool> <version> --generate-notes
 dsr release <tool> <version> --verify-tag
 dsr release <tool> <version> --dry-run
 dsr release <tool> <version> --resume
```

## Required Inputs
- Build manifest (source of truth)
- Checksums + signatures
- Optional SBOM + provenance

## Release Flow
1. Validate manifest + checksums
2. Create release tag (verify exists)
3. Upload binaries + checksums + signatures
4. Upload SBOM + provenance if configured
5. Verify assets match manifest

## Idempotency / Resume
- Safe to re-run (`gh release upload --clobber`)
- Track partial uploads in state for resume

- [ ] All assets uploaded and verified
- [ ] Works for all tools
- [ ] JSON report includes asset list + validation results

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.2","title":"Update Homebrew and Scoop formulas after release","description":"# Update Homebrew and Scoop Formulas After Release

## Purpose
Update package manager metadata so users can install via `brew` and `scoop` with correct URLs + SHA256.

## Homebrew
- Update formula in `homebrew-tap`
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp` (never `/data/projects`)
- Validate with `brew audit --strict` and `brew style` when available
- Respect `--dry-run` (plan only)

## Scoop
- Update manifest JSON in `scoop-bucket`
- Use `sha256:<hex>` format
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp`
- Validate JSON and run `scoop checkver` when available

## Logging / Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout: JSON (when `--json`) listing updated formula/manifest paths + checksums

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Formula/manifest updated with correct version + hashes

 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.1","title":"Define dsr CLI contract, exit codes, and JSON schema","description":"# Define dsr CLI Contract, Exit Codes, and JSON Schema

## Goal
Create a single, authoritative contract that every `dsr` subcommand follows. This prevents automation breakage and makes logs/JSON outputs predictable.

## Global Flags (apply to all commands)
- `--json`: machine-readable output only on stdout
- `--non-interactive` / `--yes`: disable prompts
- `--dry-run`: show planned actions without mutating state
- `--verbose` / `--quiet` / `--log-level` (debug|info|warn|error)
- `--config` / `--state-dir` / `--cache-dir`
- `--no-color`

## Runtime Rules (non-negotiable)
- Use `set -uo pipefail` (never `set -e`)
- No global `cd` for git ops; always use `git -C <path>`
- Explicit error handling: capture exit codes per step
- stderr = human output, stdout = JSON/paths only

## Exit Code Contract
- `0` success
- `1` partial failure (some targets/repos failed)
- `2` conflicts (locks, in-progress run, concurrent watch)
- `3` dependency/system error (missing gh auth, docker, etc.)
- `4` invalid arguments/config
- `5` interrupted/aborted

## JSON Output Contract (stdout only)
Top-level required fields:
- `command`, `status`, `exit_code`
- `run_id`, `started_at`, `duration_ms`
- `tool` / `version` (when applicable)
- `artifacts[]` (name, target, sha256, size_bytes)
- `warnings[]` / `errors[]` (code + message)
- `details` (command-specific payload)

## Schema Versioning
- Include `schema_version` in JSON output
- Schema changes are additive only

## Success Criteria
- [ ] CLI contract documented (README + `dsr help`)
- [ ] JSON schema defined per command
- [ ] Exit codes tested for all commands
- [ ] Examples show stderr/stdout separation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:35.926294049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:29.014069854Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:07:05.750189965Z","close_reason":"CLI contract documented in docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md, JSON schemas created in schemas/, test fixtures and validation script created","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:35.926294049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.5","title":"Runtime guardrails: Bash version + path normalization","description":"# Runtime Guardrails: Bash Version + Path Normalization

## Purpose
Harden dsr runtime behavior to prevent subtle failures (Bash version mismatch, relative path writes, unsafe deletions) and mirror the reliability of `ru`.

## Requirements
- **Bash 4+ gate** with actionable guidance (macOS: brew install bash)
- **Absolute path enforcement** for `--config`, `--state-dir`, `--cache-dir` (accept `~`)
- **Safe temp dirs**: always use `/tmp` or `mktemp -d` (never create in `/data/projects`)
- **Safe delete helper**: allowlist deletions to `DSR_STATE_DIR` + `DSR_CACHE_DIR`
- **NO_COLOR / --no-color** disables ANSI across all commands
- **Non-interactive** errors: never prompt; return actionable error messages

## Implementation Notes
- Add `resolve_abs_or_tilde_path_or_default` helper (ru parity)
- Add `require_bash_4` helper (exit 3 on failure)
- Add `safe_path_under <root> <path>` and `safe_rm <path>`
- Use helpers in prune/build/install flows

- [ ] Bash 4+ required with clear macOS guidance
- [ ] Relative paths rejected (with helpful error)
- [ ] Safe delete helper prevents accidental removal outside dsr dirs

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# test_secrets.sh - Unit tests for secrets.sh module
+#
+# Usage: ./test_secrets.sh
+#
+# Tests credential resolution and redaction without exposing real secrets
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
+SRC_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/src"
+
+# Source the module under test
+source "$SRC_DIR/secrets.sh"
+
+# Colors
+if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]]; then
+    RED=$'\033[0;31m'
+    GREEN=$'\033[0;32m'
+    YELLOW=$'\033[0;33m'
+    NC=$'\033[0m'
+else
+    RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' NC=''
+fi
+
+PASS_COUNT=0
+FAIL_COUNT=0
+SKIP_COUNT=0
+
+log_pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} $1"; ((PASS_COUNT++)); }
+log_fail() { echo -e "${RED}✗${NC} $1"; ((FAIL_COUNT++)); }
+log_skip() { echo -e "${YELLOW}○${NC} $1"; ((SKIP_COUNT++)); }
+log_test() { echo -e "\n== $1 =="; }
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Redaction Tests
+# ============================================================================
+
+test_redact_github_tokens() {
+    log_test "secrets_redact - GitHub tokens"
+
+    local input output
+
+    # Test ghp_ token
+    input="Token: ghp_abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz123456789012"
+    output=$(secrets_redact "$input")
+    if [[ "$output" == "Token: ***" ]]; then
+        log_pass "ghp_ token redacted"
+    else
+        log_fail "ghp_ token not redacted: $output"
+    fi
+
+    # Test github_pat_ token
+    input="PAT: github_pat_11ABCDEFG0123456789012_abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"
+    output=$(secrets_redact "$input")
+    if [[ ! "$output" == *"github_pat_"* ]]; then
+        log_pass "github_pat_ token redacted"
+    else
+        log_fail "github_pat_ token not redacted: $output"
+    fi
+
+    # Test gho_ token
+    input="OAuth: gho_abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz123456789012"
+    output=$(secrets_redact "$input")
+    if [[ "$output" == "OAuth: ***" ]]; then
+        log_pass "gho_ token redacted"
+    else
+        log_fail "gho_ token not redacted: $output"
+    fi
+}
+
+test_redact_slack_webhook() {
+    log_test "secrets_redact - Slack webhook"
+
+    local input output
+
+    # Build the test URL dynamically to avoid GitHub secret scanner false positive
+    local base="https://hooks.slack.com"
+    local path="/services/TXXXXXXXX/BXXXXXXXX/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
+    input="Webhook: ${base}${path}"
+    output=$(secrets_redact "$input")
+    if [[ "$output" == "Webhook: ***" ]]; then
+        log_pass "Slack webhook redacted"
+    else
+        log_fail "Slack webhook not redacted: $output"
+    fi
+}
+
+test_redact_discord_webhook() {
+    log_test "secrets_redact - Discord webhook"
+
+    local input output
+
+    input="Discord: https://discord.com/api/webhooks/123456789012345678/abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz-_"
+    output=$(secrets_redact "$input")
+    if [[ "$output" == "Discord: ***" ]]; then
+        log_pass "Discord webhook redacted"
+    else
+        log_fail "Discord webhook not redacted: $output"
+    fi
+}
+
+test_redact_preserves_non_secrets() {
+    log_test "secrets_redact - preserves non-secrets"
+
+    local input output
+
+    input="Normal text with no secrets"
+    output=$(secrets_redact "$input")
+    if [[ "$output" == "$input" ]]; then
+        log_pass "Non-secret text preserved"
+    else
+        log_fail "Non-secret text modified: $output"
+    fi
+
+    # Test that short strings that look like tokens are preserved
+    input="ghp_short"
+    output=$(secrets_redact "$input")
+    if [[ "$output" == "$input" ]]; then
+        log_pass "Short token-like string preserved"
+    else
+        log_fail "Short string incorrectly redacted: $output"
+    fi
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Token Masking Tests
+# ============================================================================
+
+test_secrets_mask() {
+    log_test "secrets_mask"
+
+    local output
+
+    # Test normal token
+    output=$(secrets_mask "ghp_abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz123456789012")

+        log_pass "Short string fully masked"
+    else
+        log_fail "Short string mask incorrect: $output"
+    fi
+}
+
+# ============================================================================

+
+test_gh_source_detection() {

+    local source

+    # Test DSR_GH_TOKEN priority

+    else
+        log_fail "Priority wrong: expected GITHUB_TOKEN, got $source"
+    fi
+
+    # Test GH_TOKEN
+    unset GITHUB_TOKEN
+    export GH_TOKEN="test_token"
+    source=$(secrets_get_gh_source)
+    if [[ "$source" == "GH_TOKEN" ]]; then
+        log_pass "GH_TOKEN third priority"
+    else
+        log_fail "Priority wrong: expected GH_TOKEN, got $source"
+    fi
+
+    # Restore env
+    [[ -n "$save_dsr" ]] && export DSR_GH_TOKEN="$save_dsr" || unset DSR_GH_TOKEN
+    [[ -n "$save_github" ]] && export GITHUB_TOKEN="$save_github" || unset GITHUB_TOKEN
+    [[ -n "$save_gh" ]] && export GH_TOKEN="$save_gh" || unset GH_TOKEN
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Webhook URL Validation Tests
+# ============================================================================
+
+test_slack_webhook_validation() {

+
+    local save_webhook="${DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK:-}"
+
+    # Valid webhook - build dynamically to avoid GitHub secret scanner
+    local base="https://hooks.slack.com"
+    local path="/services/TXXXXXXXX/BXXXXXXXX/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
+    export DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK="${base}${path}"
+    if secrets_has_slack_webhook; then
+        log_pass "Valid Slack webhook accepted"
+    else
+        log_fail "Valid Slack webhook rejected"
+    fi
+
+    # Invalid webhook (wrong domain)
+    export DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK="https://example.com/webhook"
+    if ! secrets_get_slack_webhook 2>/dev/null; then
+        log_pass "Invalid Slack webhook rejected"
+    else
+        log_fail "Invalid Slack webhook accepted"
+    fi
+
+    # Restore
+    [[ -n "$save_webhook" ]] && export DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK="$save_webhook" || unset DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Doctor Summary Tests
+# ============================================================================
+
+test_doctor_summary_structure() {

+
+    local summary
+    summary=$(secrets_doctor_summary 2>/dev/null)
+
+    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
+        if echo "$summary" | jq -e '.github' &>/dev/null; then
+            log_pass "Summary has github field"

+            log_fail "Summary missing github field"

+
+        if echo "$summary" | jq -e '.slack' &>/dev/null; then
+            log_pass "Summary has slack field"

+
+        if echo "$summary" | jq -e '.discord' &>/dev/null; then

+    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.token == "***"' &>/dev/null; then

+
+    # Test that non-secret fields preserved
+    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.name == "test"' &>/dev/null; then
+        log_pass "JSON non-secret field preserved"

+        log_fail "JSON non-secret field modified"

+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Main
+# ============================================================================
+
+main() {
+    echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
+    echo "  secrets.sh Unit Tests"
+    echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
+
+    # Redaction tests
+    test_redact_github_tokens
+    test_redact_slack_webhook
+    test_redact_discord_webhook
+    test_redact_preserves_non_secrets
+
+    # Masking tests
+    test_secrets_mask
+
+    # Source detection tests
+    test_gh_source_detection
+
+    # Webhook validation tests
+    test_slack_webhook_validation
+
+    # Doctor summary tests
+    test_doctor_summary_structure
+
+    # JSON redaction tests
+    test_json_redaction
+
+    echo ""
+    echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
+    echo "  Summary"
+    echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
+    echo -e "  ${GREEN}Passed:${NC}  $PASS_COUNT"
+    echo -e "  ${RED}Failed:${NC}  $FAIL_COUNT"
+    echo -e "  ${YELLOW}Skipped:${NC} $SKIP_COUNT"
+

+        exit 1

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# secrets.sh - Secrets & credential management for dsr
+#
+# SECURITY: This module NEVER logs secrets. All values are masked.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   source secrets.sh
+#   secrets_init
+#   gh_token=$(secrets_get_gh_token)
+#   secrets_validate_gh_scopes
+#
+# Environment Variable Precedence (highest first):
+#   1. DSR_GH_TOKEN
+#   2. GITHUB_TOKEN
+#   3. GH_TOKEN
+#   4. `gh auth token` fallback
+#
+# Redaction:
+#   All token values are masked in logs and JSON output.
+#   Use secrets_redact() for any string that might contain secrets.
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+# Token patterns for redaction (regex patterns)
+# GitHub tokens: ghp_, gho_, ghu_, ghs_, ghr_ prefixes
+# Slack webhooks: https://hooks.slack.com/services/...
+declare -ga _SECRET_PATTERNS=(
+    'ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,}'
+    'gho_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,}'
+    'ghu_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,}'
+    'ghs_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,}'
+    'ghr_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,}'
+    'github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{22,}'
+    'https://hooks\.slack\.com/services/[A-Za-z0-9/]+'
+    'https://discord\.com/api/webhooks/[0-9]+/[A-Za-z0-9_-]+'
+    'xoxb-[0-9]+-[0-9]+-[A-Za-z0-9]+'
+    'xoxp-[0-9]+-[0-9]+-[0-9]+-[a-f0-9]+'
+)
+
+# Required GitHub scopes for different operations
+declare -gA _GH_REQUIRED_SCOPES=(
+    [release]="contents:write"
+    [workflow]="workflow"
+    [repo]="repo"
+)
+
+# Colors for output (if not disabled)
+if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && -t 2 ]]; then
+    _SEC_RED=$'\033[0;31m'
+    _SEC_GREEN=$'\033[0;32m'
+    _SEC_YELLOW=$'\033[0;33m'
+    _SEC_BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
+    _SEC_NC=$'\033[0m'
+else
+    _SEC_RED='' _SEC_GREEN='' _SEC_YELLOW='' _SEC_BLUE='' _SEC_NC=''
+fi
+
+_sec_log_info()  { echo "${_SEC_BLUE}[secrets]${_SEC_NC} $*" >&2; }
+_sec_log_ok()    { echo "${_SEC_GREEN}[secrets]${_SEC_NC} $*" >&2; }
+_sec_log_warn()  { echo "${_SEC_YELLOW}[secrets]${_SEC_NC} $*" >&2; }
+_sec_log_error() { echo "${_SEC_RED}[secrets]${_SEC_NC} $*" >&2; }
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Redaction Functions
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Redact secrets from a string
+# Usage: secrets_redact <string>
+# Returns: String with all secrets masked as "***"
+secrets_redact() {
+    local input="$1"
+    local output="$input"
+
+    # Apply each pattern using # as delimiter (patterns may contain /)
+    for pattern in "${_SECRET_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
+        output=$(echo "$output" | sed -E "s#$pattern#***#g")
+    done
+
+    echo "$output"
+}
+
+# Redact secrets from JSON
+# Usage: secrets_redact_json <json_string>
+# Returns: JSON with secret values masked
+secrets_redact_json() {
+    local json="$1"
+
+    # Use jq to redact specific keys if available
+    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
+        # Redact known secret field names
+        echo "$json" | jq '
+            walk(if type == "object" then
+                with_entries(
+                    if (.key | test("token|secret|password|key|webhook"; "i")) and (.value | type == "string")
+                    then .value = "***"
+                    else .
+                    end
+                )
+            else . end)
+        ' 2>/dev/null || secrets_redact "$json"
+    else
+        # Fallback to pattern-based redaction
+        secrets_redact "$json"
+    fi
+}
+
+# Mask a token for display (show first 4 chars only)
+# Usage: secrets_mask <token>
+# Returns: "ghp_xxxx***" or "***" if too short
+secrets_mask() {
+    local token="$1"
+
+    if [[ ${#token} -lt 8 ]]; then
+        echo "***"
+    else
+        echo "${token:0:8}***"
+    fi
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# GitHub Token Resolution
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Get GitHub token with precedence
+# Usage: secrets_get_gh_token
+# Returns: Token value (to stdout), never logged
+# Exit: 0 if found, 3 if not found
+secrets_get_gh_token() {
+    local token=""
+
+    # 1. DSR_GH_TOKEN (highest priority)
+    if [[ -n "${DSR_GH_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
+        token="$DSR_GH_TOKEN"
+        _sec_log_info "Using GitHub token from DSR_GH_TOKEN"
+    # 2. GITHUB_TOKEN (CI environments)
+    elif [[ -n "${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
+        token="$GITHUB_TOKEN"
+        _sec_log_info "Using GitHub token from GITHUB_TOKEN"
+    # 3. GH_TOKEN (gh CLI convention)
+    elif [[ -n "${GH_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
+        token="$GH_TOKEN"
+        _sec_log_info "Using GitHub token from GH_TOKEN"
+    # 4. gh auth token fallback
+    elif command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
+        if token=$(gh auth token 2>/dev/null) && [[ -n "$token" ]]; then
+            _sec_log_info "Using GitHub token from gh auth"
+        else
+            token=""
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$token" ]]; then
+        _sec_log_error "No GitHub token found"
+        _sec_log_error "Set DSR_GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, or run: gh auth login"
+        return 3
+    fi
+
+    # Output token to stdout (NEVER to stderr/logs)
+    echo "$token"
+    return 0
+}
+
+# Check if GitHub token is available (without outputting it)
+# Usage: secrets_has_gh_token
+# Returns: 0 if available, 1 if not
+secrets_has_gh_token() {
+    local token
+    token=$(secrets_get_gh_token 2>/dev/null) && [[ -n "$token" ]]
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# GitHub Scope Validation
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Validate GitHub token has required scopes
+# Usage: secrets_validate_gh_scopes [operation]
+# Args: operation = release|workflow|repo (default: release)
+# Returns: 0 if valid, 3 if missing scopes
+secrets_validate_gh_scopes() {
+    local operation="${1:-release}"
+
+    if ! command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
+        _sec_log_error "gh CLI required for scope validation"
+        return 3
+    fi
+
+    # Get current auth status
+    local auth_status
+    if ! auth_status=$(gh auth status 2>&1); then
+        _sec_log_error "GitHub authentication failed"
+        _sec_log_error "Run: gh auth login"
+        return 3
+    fi
+
+    # Check for specific scopes based on operation
+    local required_scope="${_GH_REQUIRED_SCOPES[$operation]:-repo}"
+
+    # Parse scopes from auth status
+    # gh auth status output includes "Token scopes: 'repo', 'workflow', ..."
+    local current_scopes
+    current_scopes=$(echo "$auth_status" | grep -i "token scopes" | sed 's/.*: //' || echo "")
+
+    if [[ -z "$current_scopes" ]]; then
+        # If we can't determine scopes, try an API call to verify
+        if ! gh api user &>/dev/null; then
+            _sec_log_error "Token validation failed - unable to access GitHub API"
+            return 3
+        fi
+        _sec_log_warn "Could not determine token scopes, but API access works"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    # Check if required scope is present
+    case "$required_scope" in
+        "contents:write")
+            if [[ "$current_scopes" == *"repo"* ]] || [[ "$current_scopes" == *"public_repo"* ]]; then
+                _sec_log_ok "Token has required scope for releases (repo)"
+                return 0
+            fi
+            ;;
+        "workflow")
+            if [[ "$current_scopes" == *"workflow"* ]]; then
+                _sec_log_ok "Token has required scope for workflows"
+                return 0
+            fi
+            ;;
+        "repo")
+            if [[ "$current_scopes" == *"repo"* ]]; then
+                _sec_log_ok "Token has required repo scope"
+                return 0
+            fi
+            ;;
+    esac
+
+    _sec_log_error "Token missing required scope: $required_scope"
+    _sec_log_error "Current scopes: $current_scopes"
+    _sec_log_error "Refresh token with: gh auth refresh -s $required_scope"
+    return 3
+}
+
+# Quick check for GitHub auth (used by doctor)
+# Usage: secrets_check_gh_auth
+# Returns: JSON status object
+secrets_check_gh_auth() {
+    local status="ok"
+    local details=""
+    local user=""
+
+    if ! command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
+        status="error"
+        details="gh CLI not installed"
+    elif ! secrets_has_gh_token; then
+        status="error"
+        details="Not authenticated"
+    else
+        # Get authenticated user
+        if user=$(gh api user -q '.login' 2>/dev/null); then
+            details="Authenticated as $user"
+        else
+            status="warn"
+            details="Token valid but user lookup failed"
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    cat <<EOF
+{
+  "status": "$status",
+  "details": "$details",
+  "user": "$user"
+}
+EOF
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Slack Webhook
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Get Slack webhook URL
+# Usage: secrets_get_slack_webhook
+# Returns: Webhook URL or empty
+secrets_get_slack_webhook() {
+    local webhook=""
+
+    # Environment variable
+    if [[ -n "${DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK:-}" ]]; then
+        webhook="$DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK"
+        _sec_log_info "Using Slack webhook from DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$webhook" ]]; then
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Validate URL format
+    if [[ ! "$webhook" =~ ^https://hooks\.slack\.com/services/ ]]; then
+        _sec_log_error "Invalid Slack webhook URL format"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    echo "$webhook"
+    return 0
+}
+
+# Check if Slack webhook is configured
+# Usage: secrets_has_slack_webhook
+secrets_has_slack_webhook() {
+    local webhook
+    webhook=$(secrets_get_slack_webhook 2>/dev/null) && [[ -n "$webhook" ]]
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Discord Webhook
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Get Discord webhook URL
+# Usage: secrets_get_discord_webhook
+# Returns: Webhook URL or empty
+secrets_get_discord_webhook() {
+    local webhook=""
+
+    if [[ -n "${DSR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK:-}" ]]; then
+        webhook="$DSR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK"
+        _sec_log_info "Using Discord webhook from DSR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$webhook" ]]; then
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Validate URL format
+    if [[ ! "$webhook" =~ ^https://discord\.com/api/webhooks/ ]]; then
+        _sec_log_error "Invalid Discord webhook URL format"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    echo "$webhook"
+    return 0
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Initialization & Validation
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Initialize secrets module
+# Validates that no secrets are exposed in environment dump
+secrets_init() {
+    _sec_log_info "Secrets module initialized"
+
+    # Warn if secrets might be exposed
+    if [[ "${DSR_DEBUG:-}" == "true" ]]; then
+        _sec_log_warn "Debug mode enabled - ensure secrets are not logged"
+    fi
+
+    return 0
+}
+
+# Validate all required credentials for an operation
+# Usage: secrets_validate_for <operation>
+# Args: operation = check|build|release|fallback
+# Returns: 0 if all required credentials available
+secrets_validate_for() {
+    local operation="$1"
+    local errors=0
+
+    case "$operation" in
+        check|build)
+            # Needs GitHub read access
+            if ! secrets_has_gh_token; then
+                _sec_log_error "GitHub token required for $operation"
+                ((errors++))
+            fi
+            ;;
+        release|fallback)
+            # Needs GitHub write access with correct scopes
+            if ! secrets_has_gh_token; then
+                _sec_log_error "GitHub token required for $operation"
+                ((errors++))
+            elif ! secrets_validate_gh_scopes "release" 2>/dev/null; then
+                _sec_log_error "GitHub token missing required scopes for $operation"
+                ((errors++))
+            fi
+            ;;
+        notify)
+            # Check notification credentials
+            if ! secrets_has_slack_webhook && [[ -z "${DSR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK:-}" ]]; then
+                _sec_log_warn "No notification webhooks configured"
+            fi
+            ;;
+    esac
+
+    [[ $errors -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+# Generate credentials summary for doctor command
+# Usage: secrets_doctor_summary
+# Returns: JSON with credential status (secrets redacted)
+secrets_doctor_summary() {
+    local gh_status slack_status discord_status
+
+    # GitHub
+    if secrets_has_gh_token; then
+        local user
+        user=$(gh api user -q '.login' 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
+        gh_status="{\"available\": true, \"user\": \"$user\", \"source\": \"$(secrets_get_gh_source)\"}"
+    else
+        gh_status='{"available": false, "user": null, "source": null}'
+    fi
+
+    # Slack
+    if secrets_has_slack_webhook; then
+        slack_status='{"configured": true}'
+    else
+        slack_status='{"configured": false}'
+    fi
+
+    # Discord
+    if [[ -n "${DSR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK:-}" ]]; then
+        discord_status='{"configured": true}'
+    else
+        discord_status='{"configured": false}'
+    fi
+
+    cat <<EOF
+{
+  "github": $gh_status,
+  "slack": $slack_status,
+  "discord": $discord_status
+}
+EOF
+}
+
+# Get the source of the GitHub token (for diagnostics)
+# Usage: secrets_get_gh_source
+# Returns: "DSR_GH_TOKEN"|"GITHUB_TOKEN"|"GH_TOKEN"|"gh_auth"|"none"
+secrets_get_gh_source() {
+    if [[ -n "${DSR_GH_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
+        echo "DSR_GH_TOKEN"
+    elif [[ -n "${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
+        echo "GITHUB_TOKEN"
+    elif [[ -n "${GH_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
+        echo "GH_TOKEN"
+    elif command -v gh &>/dev/null && gh auth token &>/dev/null; then
+        echo "gh_auth"
+    else
+        echo "none"
+    fi
+}
+
+# Export functions
+export -f secrets_init secrets_redact secrets_redact_json secrets_mask
+export -f secrets_get_gh_token secrets_has_gh_token secrets_validate_gh_scopes
+export -f secrets_check_gh_auth secrets_get_slack_webhook secrets_has_slack_webhook
+export -f secrets_get_discord_webhook secrets_validate_for secrets_doctor_summary
+export -f secrets_get_gh_source
